{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Supply Chain Manager",
  "description": "ISN Medical is the leading supplier of Medical Diagnostic Products and Services in Nigeria. Our mission is to give healthcare practitioners and patients peace of mind through innovative medical diagnostic solutions.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Supply Chain Manager

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Employment Type: Full Time

About the Job


	We are seeking a results-driven and strategic Supply Chain Manager to lead end-to-end supply chain operations across planning, procurement, warehousing, logistics, and regulatory coordination.
	The role will ensure product availability, cost efficiency, and seamless delivery across all business units, while strengthening supply chain resilience and supporting business growth.


Key Responsibilities


	Lead demand forecasting and supply planning to ensure optimal product availability.
	Align supply plans with sales forecasts, business targets, and market trends.
	Drive inventory optimization to balance availability with working capital efficiency.
	Oversee procurement processes to ensure cost-effective and timely sourcing of products and services.
	Manage relationships with OEMs, suppliers, and vendors to ensure reliability and performance.
	Negotiate contracts and monitor supplier performance against agreed SLAs.
	Ensure efficient warehouse operations, including stock accuracy, storage, and distribution.
	Implement inventory control measures to minimise stock-outs, overstocking, and expiries.
	Drive adherence to inventory policies and cycle count processes.
	Manage logistics operations to ensure timely and cost-effective delivery to customers and business units.
	Optimise transportation routes, delivery timelines, and third-party logistics performance.
	Monitor and improve delivery turnaround times and service levels.
	Ensure compliance with all regulatory requirements related to importation, storage, and distribution.
	Work closely with regulatory bodies and internal quality teams to maintain compliance standards.
	Support audit readiness and documentation for regulatory and quality inspections.
	Establish and monitor supply chain KPIs (inventory days, fill rate, OTIF, order cycle time).
	Drive continuous improvement initiatives to enhance efficiency and reduce costs.
	Leverage data and systems to improve visibility and decision-making across the supply chain.


Qualifications


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Supply Chain Management, Engineering, Business Administration, or a related field. Minimum of Second Class Upper (2:1) for Bachelor&rsquo;s degree holders or Upper Credit for HND holders is required.
	6&ndash;10 years of experience in supply chain, logistics, or operations management.
	Professional certification such as CSCP, CPIM, CIPS, or equivalent is an added advantage.
	Strong understanding of demand planning, inventory management, procurement, and logistics.
	Experience working with ERP systems (e.g., D365) and data analysis tools.&nbsp;


Must have skills:


	Supply chain management
	Leadership and team management
	Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
	Strategic thinking with operational execution ability
	Effective stakeholder and vendor management
	High attention to detail and process discipline
	Results-driven with a continuous improvement mindset.
",
  "datePosted": "2026-04-10",
  "validThrough": "2026-05-11",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",
  "hiringOrganization": {
    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "ISN Medical Incorporated"
    
  },

  },

      "minValue": 0,
      "maxValue": 0,

}



Supply Chain Manager Job at ISN Medical




        
    

        
        ISN Medical is the leading supplier of Medical Diagnostic Products and Services in Nigeria. Our mission is to give healthcare practitioners and patients peace of mind through innovative medical diagnostic solutions.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Supply Chain Manager

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full Time

About the Job
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a results-driven and strategic Supply Chain Manager to lead end-to-end supply chain operations across planning, procurement, warehousing, logistics, and regulatory coordination.
	The role will ensure product availability, cost efficiency, and seamless delivery across all business units, while strengthening supply chain resilience and supporting business growth.


Key Responsibilities


	Lead demand forecasting and supply planning to ensure optimal product availability.
	Align supply plans with sales forecasts, business targets, and market trends.
	Drive inventory optimization to balance availability with working capital efficiency.
	Oversee procurement processes to ensure cost-effective and timely sourcing of products and services.
	Manage relationships with OEMs, suppliers, and vendors to ensure reliability and performance.
	Negotiate contracts and monitor supplier performance against agreed SLAs.
	Ensure efficient warehouse operations, including stock accuracy, storage, and distribution.
	Implement inventory control measures to minimise stock-outs, overstocking, and expiries.
	Drive adherence to inventory policies and cycle count processes.
	Manage logistics operations to ensure timely and cost-effective delivery to customers and business units.
	Optimise transportation routes, delivery timelines, and third-party logistics performance.
	Monitor and improve delivery turnaround times and service levels.
	Ensure compliance with all regulatory requirements related to importation, storage, and distribution.
	Work closely with regulatory bodies and internal quality teams to maintain compliance standards.
	Support audit readiness and documentation for regulatory and quality inspections.
	Establish and monitor supply chain KPIs (inventory days, fill rate, OTIF, order cycle time).
	Drive continuous improvement initiatives to enhance efficiency and reduce costs.
	Leverage data and systems to improve visibility and decision-making across the supply chain.


Qualifications


	Bachelor’s Degree in Supply Chain Management, Engineering, Business Administration, or a related field. Minimum of Second Class Upper (2:1) for Bachelor’s degree holders or Upper Credit for HND holders is required.
	6–10 years of experience in supply chain, logistics, or operations management.
	Professional certification such as CSCP, CPIM, CIPS, or equivalent is an added advantage.
	Strong understanding of demand planning, inventory management, procurement, and logistics.
	Experience working with ERP systems (e.g., D365) and data analysis tools. 


Must have skills:


	Supply chain management
	Leadership and team management
	Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
	Strategic thinking with operational execution ability
	Effective stakeholder and vendor management
	High attention to detail and process discipline
	Results-driven with a continuous improvement mindset.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "description": "Multipro Consumer Products Limited is the largest company in Nigeria dealing in Sales, Haulage and Logistics. MCPL was founded in 1996 as a fully owned company of the Tolaram Group. The first product sold by MCPL in 1996 was Indomie and since then it has played a seminal role in making Indomie a household brand in Nigeria. MCPL over the years has grown adding 13 brands in various categories comprising of Noodle, Snacks, Milk, Cereal, Oil, Homecare and Personal Hygiene.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Receptionist

Job Location:&nbsp;Victoria Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The lounge receptionist serves as the first point of contact for guests, ensuring a warm welcome and a smooth, enjoyable experience.


Key Responsibilities


	Greet and welcome guests promptly and courteously
	Manage reservations, bookings, and walk-in guests
	Answer calls and respond to customer inquiries
	Escort guests to their seats or reserved sections
	Coordinate with service staff to ensure efficient guest flow
	Handle customer concerns or complaints professionally
	Maintain a clean and organized reception area.
	Monitor guest traffic and assist in crowd control when necessary.


Qualifications


	Minimum of an HND / BSc Degree or equivalent
	Previous experience in hospitality, customer service, or a similar role is an advantage
	Ability to work flexible hours, including evenings and weekends.


Requirements:


	Excellent communication and interpersonal skills
	Friendly, well-groomed, and professional appearance
	Ability to multitask in a fast-paced environment
	Basic computer skills or experience with booking systems
	Strong customer service orientation.


Salary
Competitive + Incentives.",

Receptionist Job at Multipro Consumer Products Limited




        
    

        
        Multipro Consumer Products Limited is the largest company in Nigeria dealing in Sales, Haulage and Logistics. MCPL was founded in 1996 as a fully owned company of the Tolaram Group. The first product sold by MCPL in 1996 was Indomie and since then it has played a seminal role in making Indomie a household brand in Nigeria. MCPL over the years has grown adding 13 brands in various categories comprising of Noodle, Snacks, Milk, Cereal, Oil, Homecare and Personal Hygiene.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Receptionist

Job Location: Victoria Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The lounge receptionist serves as the first point of contact for guests, ensuring a warm welcome and a smooth, enjoyable experience.


Key Responsibilities


	Greet and welcome guests promptly and courteously
	Manage reservations, bookings, and walk-in guests
	Answer calls and respond to customer inquiries
	Escort guests to their seats or reserved sections
	Coordinate with service staff to ensure efficient guest flow
	Handle customer concerns or complaints professionally
	Maintain a clean and organized reception area.
	Monitor guest traffic and assist in crowd control when necessary.


Qualifications


	Minimum of an HND / BSc Degree or equivalent
	Previous experience in hospitality, customer service, or a similar role is an advantage
	Ability to work flexible hours, including evenings and weekends.


Requirements:


	Excellent communication and interpersonal skills
	Friendly, well-groomed, and professional appearance
	Ability to multitask in a fast-paced environment
	Basic computer skills or experience with booking systems
	Strong customer service orientation.


Salary
Competitive + Incentives.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Head of Procurement",
  "description": "WTS Energy - Our client&nbsp;is recruiting suitable candidates to fill the position below:

Job Position: Head of Procurement

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos

Job Summary


	Our client is seeking a Head of Procurement to lead the procurement and supply chain functions critical to rig operations and oilfield servicing activities. This role is within a dynamic oilfield services environment focused on delivering timely, cost-effective, and compliant acquisition of goods and services.
	The Head of Procurement will develop strategic sourcing plans, manage vendor relationships, negotiate contracts, and ensure adherence to industry regulations and company policies.
	The position requires overseeing end-to-end procurement operations while collaborating closely with cross-functional teams to maintain seamless supply chain support. Candidates can expect to provide leadership to a procurement team, drive continuous process improvements, and mitigate procurement risks in a fast-paced, safety-conscious setting.


Responsibilities


	Develop and execute Our Client&rsquo;s procurement strategy to align with operational goals and project timelines, ensuring cost-effective and timely acquisition of goods and services
	Forecast procurement requirements and establish detailed procurement plans to support rig operations and drilling activities
	Build and maintain a robust vendor network for drilling equipment, rig supplies, spare parts, logistics, and technical services, ensuring high standards of quality and compliance
	Lead contract negotiations to secure favorable terms, pricing, and service delivery while managing supplier contracts to ensure adherence to agreed conditions
	Oversee the end-to-end procurement process, including purchase requisitions, RFQs, bid evaluations, purchase order issuance, delivery coordination, and payment processing
	Collaborate cross-functionally with Operations, Engineering, Finance, and Logistics teams to ensure seamless supply chain support and monitor inventory levels effectively
	Enforce strict compliance with Our Client&rsquo;s procurement policies, industry regulations, and ethical sourcing standards, including anti-bribery and anti-corruption measures
	Identify and mitigate procurement risks such as supply delays, cost fluctuations, and supplier dependency through proactive risk management strategies
	Provide strong leadership and mentorship to the procurement team, fostering a culture of accountability, integrity, and continuous improvement in procurement processes and systems


Qualifications
Required Qualifications:


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in Supply Chain Management, Engineering, Business Administration, or a related field
	10-15 years of experience in procurement and supply chain management, including at least 5 years in a senior leadership role within the oilfield servicing or upstream oil and gas sector
	Strong knowledge of procurement best practices specific to oil and gas rig operations and well services
	Proven expertise in strategic sourcing, vendor management, contract negotiation, and compliance with industry regulations
	In-depth understanding of supply chain management, incoterms, and logistics within oilfield servicing environments
	Demonstrated financial and commercial acumen with the ability to evaluate bids, manage budgets, and optimize cost savings
	Proficiency in ERP and procurement software such as SAP, Oracle, or Microsoft Dynamics
	Excellent leadership, communication, and stakeholder management skills to effectively lead and develop procurement teams


Preferred Qualifications


	Master&rsquo;s degree, MBA, or MSc in Supply Chain, Logistics, Business Administration, or a related discipline
	Professional certifications such as CIPS, Chartered Institute of Procurement and Supply, CPSM Certified Professional in Supply Management, or PMP, Project Management Professional, for project-related procurement
	Experience driving continuous improvement initiatives in procurement processes and systems
	Strong commitment to governance, ethical sourcing, and compliance with anti-bribery and anti-corruption policies.


What We Offer:


	Comprehensive professional development opportunities including mentorship and continuous improvement initiatives to enhance procurement expertise and leadership skills
	A dynamic work environment within the oilfield servicing sector, offering exposure to strategic sourcing, vendor management, and contract negotiation at a senior leadership level
	Collaborative culture promoting cross-functional teamwork with Operations, Engineering, Finance, and Logistics departments to ensure seamless supply chain support
	Commitment to ethical procurement practices, compliance with industry regulations, and a strong focus on health, safety, and environmental standards
	Leadership role with the opportunity to shape procurement strategy, drive operational excellence, and influence risk management in a critical business function
	Access to advanced procurement technologies and ERP systems to support efficient and effective supply chain management.
",
  "datePosted": "2026-04-10",
  "validThrough": "2026-05-11",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",
  "hiringOrganization": {
    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "WTS Energy"
    
  },

      "addressCountry": "NG"
     
      
    }

Head of Procurement Job at WTS Energy




        
    

        
        WTS Energy - Our client is recruiting suitable candidates to fill the position below:

Job Position: Head of Procurement

Job Location: Lagos

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Our client is seeking a Head of Procurement to lead the procurement and supply chain functions critical to rig operations and oilfield servicing activities. This role is within a dynamic oilfield services environment focused on delivering timely, cost-effective, and compliant acquisition of goods and services.
	The Head of Procurement will develop strategic sourcing plans, manage vendor relationships, negotiate contracts, and ensure adherence to industry regulations and company policies.
	The position requires overseeing end-to-end procurement operations while collaborating closely with cross-functional teams to maintain seamless supply chain support. Candidates can expect to provide leadership to a procurement team, drive continuous process improvements, and mitigate procurement risks in a fast-paced, safety-conscious setting.


Responsibilities


	Develop and execute Our Client’s procurement strategy to align with operational goals and project timelines, ensuring cost-effective and timely acquisition of goods and services
	Forecast procurement requirements and establish detailed procurement plans to support rig operations and drilling activities
	Build and maintain a robust vendor network for drilling equipment, rig supplies, spare parts, logistics, and technical services, ensuring high standards of quality and compliance
	Lead contract negotiations to secure favorable terms, pricing, and service delivery while managing supplier contracts to ensure adherence to agreed conditions
	Oversee the end-to-end procurement process, including purchase requisitions, RFQs, bid evaluations, purchase order issuance, delivery coordination, and payment processing
	Collaborate cross-functionally with Operations, Engineering, Finance, and Logistics teams to ensure seamless supply chain support and monitor inventory levels effectively
	Enforce strict compliance with Our Client’s procurement policies, industry regulations, and ethical sourcing standards, including anti-bribery and anti-corruption measures
	Identify and mitigate procurement risks such as supply delays, cost fluctuations, and supplier dependency through proactive risk management strategies
	Provide strong leadership and mentorship to the procurement team, fostering a culture of accountability, integrity, and continuous improvement in procurement processes and systems


Qualifications
Required Qualifications:


	Bachelor’s degree in Supply Chain Management, Engineering, Business Administration, or a related field
	10-15 years of experience in procurement and supply chain management, including at least 5 years in a senior leadership role within the oilfield servicing or upstream oil and gas sector
	Strong knowledge of procurement best practices specific to oil and gas rig operations and well services
	Proven expertise in strategic sourcing, vendor management, contract negotiation, and compliance with industry regulations
	In-depth understanding of supply chain management, incoterms, and logistics within oilfield servicing environments
	Demonstrated financial and commercial acumen with the ability to evaluate bids, manage budgets, and optimize cost savings
	Proficiency in ERP and procurement software such as SAP, Oracle, or Microsoft Dynamics
	Excellent leadership, communication, and stakeholder management skills to effectively lead and develop procurement teams


Preferred Qualifications


	Master’s degree, MBA, or MSc in Supply Chain, Logistics, Business Administration, or a related discipline
	Professional certifications such as CIPS, Chartered Institute of Procurement and Supply, CPSM Certified Professional in Supply Management, or PMP, Project Management Professional, for project-related procurement
	Experience driving continuous improvement initiatives in procurement processes and systems
	Strong commitment to governance, ethical sourcing, and compliance with anti-bribery and anti-corruption policies.


What We Offer:


	Comprehensive professional development opportunities including mentorship and continuous improvement initiatives to enhance procurement expertise and leadership skills
	A dynamic work environment within the oilfield servicing sector, offering exposure to strategic sourcing, vendor management, and contract negotiation at a senior leadership level
	Collaborative culture promoting cross-functional teamwork with Operations, Engineering, Finance, and Logistics departments to ensure seamless supply chain support
	Commitment to ethical procurement practices, compliance with industry regulations, and a strong focus on health, safety, and environmental standards
	Leadership role with the opportunity to shape procurement strategy, drive operational excellence, and influence risk management in a critical business function
	Access to advanced procurement technologies and ERP systems to support efficient and effective supply chain management.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

Business Development Manager Job at Westfield Consulting Limited




        
    

        
        Westfield Consulting Limited, a consulting firm in the Human Resources space, is looking for qualified and passionate candidates to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Development Manager

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Purpose


	Creating awareness and the successive selling/upselling and cross selling of our services to prospective clients.
	Relationship Management of existing clients so they continue doing business with us.


Key Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Build relationship with key customers
	Identify business growth opportunities of company services
	Development of strategies to increase sales of company services
	Improve company services being patronized by existing clientele
	Getting of customers feedback to determine whether customers are satisfied with company services
	Conducting market research to identify new business opportunities
	Collaborating with company executives to determine the most viable, cost-effective approach to pursue new business opportunities
	Exploring the cross-selling possibilities of company services to both new and existing clients
	Ensuring improve social media presence aimed at promoting company services
	Other duties and responsibilities as assigned from time to time in line with overall job purpose


Requirements


	First Degree in a Numerate or Business-related discipline
	Minimum of 2 years of cognate work experience in Human Resources / Business Development
	Experience in Consulting is an added advantage
	Strong networking ability
	Knowledge of company services and the ability to sell them.


Salary
N200,000 – N350, 000 monthly net.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their curent CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.    

    

        Click here to Apply
